name: FEniCSx (Finite Element Computation)
pricing_notes: Open source (LGPL-3.0); FEniCS Project / Baratta, Dean, Dokken et al.

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required — complex install (MPI, PETSc, HDF5)

performance.notes: MPI parallel; large PDE problems run hours/days on HPC clusters

agent_readiness.notes: Finite element method for solving PDEs. from dolfinx import mesh, fem, io. from dolfinx.fem import functionspace, Function. from ufl import *. domain = mesh.create_unit_square(MPI.COMM_WORLD, 32, 32). V = functionspace(domain, ('Lagrange', 1)). u = ufl.TrialFunction(V); v = ufl.TestFunction(V). a = inner(grad(u), grad(v)) * dx; L = inner(f, v) * dx. uh = fem.Function(V). fem.petsc.LinearProblem(a, L, bcs, u=uh).solve(). io.XDMFFile: write/read. dolfinx.plot.vtk_mesh for PyVista. Gmsh mesh import. Navier-Stokes, elasticity, heat, electromagnetics.

gotchas: Complex install: requires PETSc, MPI, HDF5, SCOTCH — Docker/conda recommended
FEniCS legacy vs FEniCSx (dolfinx): incompatible APIs — use FEniCSx (dolfinx) for new work
Steep learning curve — requires FEM theory (weak forms, function spaces, BCs)
UFL symbolic math for variational formulations is not beginner-friendly
MPI parallel execution — single-node code differs from parallel
best_when: Solving PDEs: Poisson, Navier-Stokes, linear elasticity, heat equation, Maxwell
When academic/research-grade FEM solver is needed with Python scripting
Complex multiphysics with custom variational formulations
HPC PDE simulation with MPI parallelism
avoid_when: Simple ODE/SDE problems (use scipy.integrate or sympy)
When commercial FEA (ANSYS, ABAQUS, COMSOL) is required
summary: FEniCSx (dolfinx) solves PDEs using finite element method. functionspace(), TrialFunction/TestFunction, UFL variational forms, PETSc linear solver. Poisson, Navier-Stokes, elasticity. MPI parallel. Gmsh mesh import. XDMF output. LGPL-3.0 by FEniCS Project. Gold standard Python FEM for research-grade PDE solving. Complex install (Docker recommended). Steep FEM theory prerequisite. UFL symbolic forms. FEniCS legacy vs FEniCSx are incompatible.
